evals.prompt: I want to add a constraint that sub-agents must not modify files outside their worktree. Should this be a rule, a command step, or a hook?
evals.expected_output: Routes to hook as primary enforcement (deterministic, exit code). May keep a one-line rule if understanding the constraint changes pre-enforcement behavior. Should NOT recommend a command step.
expectations: Identifies hook as the correct primary layer
Explains why a rule alone is insufficient (prose is non-deterministic)
Does not recommend embedding this as a command step
References the taxonomy decision tree

evals.prompt: Write a new command for running connectivity tests across all checkpoints after a batch completes.
evals.expected_output: A complete command file following the structural template: YAML frontmatter, chain positioning, state initialization, procedural steps with at least one gate, constraints section.
expectations: Output has YAML frontmatter with name and description
Includes chain positioning diagram
Has at least one HARD GATE or HUMAN GATE
Steps are verifiable (each step names its verification)
Includes earned-prose justification for each step (why the step earns its place)

evals.prompt: Review navigation.md and tell me which lines should be deleted or moved to another layer.
evals.expected_output: A review in the findings table format. Should identify that the rule is well-constructed (cost-first, concise) but may flag specific lines that restate tool mechanics rather than cost reasoning.
expectations: Traces each flagged line through all 3 filter questions (hook-enforceable, behavior-before-enforcement, WHAT-vs-WHY)
Applies the 3-question earned-prose filter
Recognizes the cost premise pattern as correct
Distinguishes between lines that pass the filter cleanly vs lines where cost phrasing could be more explicit

evals.prompt: Review the minimal-coder skill and identify any prose that isn't earning its place.
evals.expected_output: A review applying the skill earned-prose filter. Should evaluate whether each section reduces output variance or restates general model knowledge.
expectations: Traces each section through all 3 skill filter questions (variance, inference, constraint-vs-trigger)
Applies the 3-question skill filter (variance, inference, constraint vs trigger)
Evaluates the Permissions section against the variance test
Does not recommend deleting the Required Input section (high variance reduction)

evals.prompt: Review planning.md and tell me what should change.
evals.expected_output: A review that identifies the DESIGN PRINCIPLES section as general knowledge restated (an experienced model already knows loose coupling, DI, testability). Should flag the ANTI-PATTERNS section similarly. Should keep the [HARD] State Management section (project-specific cost reasoning about the Living Document). Verdict should be TRIM, not RESTRUCTURE or RELOCATE.
expectations: Flags DESIGN PRINCIPLES as general knowledge the model already knows (anti-pattern #5)
Keeps the [HARD] State Management section as project-specific cost reasoning
Traces key sections through the 3-question rule filter
Verdict is TRIM (not RESTRUCTURE or RELOCATE)
Does not recommend deleting the context blockquote (scopes the rule to design phase)

evals.prompt: Review scope-discipline.md and tell me what should change.
evals.expected_output: A review concluding CLEAN or near-CLEAN. The file is well-constructed: cost reasoning is explicit, constraints shape pre-enforcement behavior, and nothing restates hook enforcement. Minor suggestions acceptable but verdict should not be RESTRUCTURE or RELOCATE.
expectations: Verdict is CLEAN or TRIM with minor suggestions only
Traces key lines through the 3-question rule filter
Recognizes that the constraints shape behavior before enforcement (passes Q2)
Does not recommend deleting the Revert Ban or Assumption Ban (high cost-reasoning value)
Does not recommend relocating content to another layer
